metadata.title:
GROWTH OF UNION LABOR


IN SPOKANE.

pages.raw_text:
According to statements made last
evening at Central Labor hail by
some of those who are prominently
connected with the promotion of
trades uniunism, the progress of the
labor movement in Spokane within
the past year is unprecedented.


with the exception of n few of
the international bodies. Spokane two
years ago was one OF the most poor
~ organized cities in the country
and was advertised far and wide as
the very hothcd of non-lnionsim Its
reputation. however, has SO complete
~ changed that national organizers
who have visited the city have CON
graduated the trades council upon
the fact that the work of organza
tion is in Il more iIUurishing condi-
tion here than in any other city in
the country.


"Such llattering words said the
secretary of the council, Horace
Brown, coming from the lips of
men who have been thoroughly idcn-
titled with the cause of labor for
many years, are certainly to be
priced. At the present time every
craft is represented in the trades
council and they are SO completely
organized that a nonunion man is
very hard to find"


j. c. Daily, organizer for the
